title: dodag.rs: select_parent hysteresis ignores current parent admissibility
description:
File: rust/lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:261-270

In select_parent(), the hysteresis logic compares the current parent's cost against the best admissible candidate but doesn't verify that the current parent itself is still admissible.

The code at lines 261-270:
```rust
let (chosen_addr, chosen_cost) = match self.preferred_parent {
    Some(cur_addr) if cur_addr != best_addr => {
        // Check if current parent still exists and improvement is below threshold.
        self.parents
            .get(&cur_addr)
            .map(|cur| cur.path_cost(mhri))
            .filter(|&cur_cost| best_cost > cur_cost.saturating_sub(threshold))
            .map_or((best_addr, best_cost), |cur_cost| (cur_addr, cur_cost))
    }
    _ => (best_addr, best_cost),
};
```

If the current parent's rank increased (e.g., from a DIO with higher rank), it could become inadmissible (path_cost > lowest_rank + max_rank_increase) while still being in the parent HashMap. The hysteresis check would still prefer staying with it if the improvement doesn't exceed threshold.

This violates RFC 6550 Section 8.2.2.4: 'A node MUST NOT increase its own rank beyond a ceiling value DAGMaxRankIncrease + cur_min_path_cost'.

Fix: Add admissible() check before applying hysteresis:
```rust
.get(&cur_addr)
.filter(|cur| self.admissible(cur))
.map(|cur| cur.path_cost(mhri))
```

Note: This is the same bug that was fixed in the C port (lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/dodag.c).
close_reason: Added admissibility check to Rust parent hysteresis and regression test; focused tests and library clippy pass.
